review_reason: NoMaxOrder/NoMinOrder on ConstructiveQuotient cannot be proven with current approach. The Lindenbaum construction is non-constructive and cannot guarantee strict successors. G-complete MCSs (where phi in M iff G(phi) in M) are consistent with all axioms, creating quotient classes with no strict successor. This is the same blocker found in Boneyard/RestrictedFragment.lean:446-463. Recommend product/bulldozing construction (Option A in summary).

partial_progress.details: Phase 2B blocked: NoMaxOrder/NoMinOrder on ConstructiveQuotient requires showing forward witnesses produce strictly greater elements, but Lindenbaum non-constructivity prevents this. G-complete MCSs (phi in M iff G(phi) in M) are consistent with all axioms, meaning the quotient CAN be a singleton, making NoMaxOrder false. The temporal T-axiom (G(phi) -> phi) is unsound for strict-future semantics (G quantifies over s > t). Previous Boneyard approach used product construction (bulldozing) to sidestep this.

errors.message: NoMaxOrder on ConstructiveQuotient: G-complete MCS blocker. Same as Boneyard/RestrictedFragment.lean:446-463.
errors.recommendation: Adopt product/bulldozing construction from Boneyard/TemporalDomain.lean: define D as ConstructiveQuotient x Q, get NoMaxOrder from Q component.

completion_data.completion_summary: Phase 2B NoMaxOrder/NoMinOrder blocked by G-complete MCS problem. Extensive analysis of all 15 axiom schemata confirms this is a genuine mathematical obstacle, not a tactic gap. The Boneyard independently discovered and documented the same blocker.
